critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:
1. DEMAND_RESPONSE_CONFOUND: Load curve changed between years due to interruptible contracts and TOU tariff — possibly easier to forecast.
2. NONSTATIONARY_TARGET_PROCESS: TOU tariff shifted cooling behavior; the forecasting problem changed between years.
3. WEATHER_INPUT_MISMATCH: Better weather vendor is an input quality advantage, not a methodology advantage.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (seeing critique):
On demand_response_confound: I initially argue the new environment is the operational reality. But I concede: if the load curve is genuinely flatter and more predictable due to demand response, then MAPE improvement partly reflects an easier problem, not a better model. The claim 'better modeling approach' requires evidence on the same problem.

On nonstationary_target_process: Conceded. TOU-shifted demand behavior changes the forecast target. Year-over-year error comparison is only valid if the process is stationary.

On weather_input_mismatch: Partially rebutted — better weather data is operationally relevant. But I concede that for evaluating methodology specifically, inputs must be held constant.

Proposed resolution: evaluate both models on the same historical periods with identical weather vendor data. If the new model is genuinely better, it will show improvement under controlled conditions.
adjudication_raw: After round 1, all three issues are resolved with the defender's concessions. Empirical test agreed: matched historical evaluation with identical inputs is required to establish methodology improvement.

debate_rounds.defender_response: Conceded all three. Better weather data is operationally relevant but held constant needed for methodology evaluation.

empirical_test.measure: MAPE on the same historical summer intervals for both models with identical weather inputs; separate analysis for curtailment days
empirical_test.success_criterion: New model shows no consistent MAPE improvement on matched historical intervals — confirming demand-response environment change explains the year-over-year gap
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New model consistently improves on matched intervals — confirming genuine methodology improvement independent of environment and input changes
